title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (May 13)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: A one-family Colonial in Arlington sold for $2,150,000, featuring 6 bedrooms and 4 baths.
A contemporary home in Ashland with 3 bedrooms and 4 baths sold for $500,000.
A high-rise condo in Boston at 1 Dalton St. sold for $4,900,000, with 2 bedrooms and 3 baths.

title: North Adams man convicted, sentenced for murdering his wife in 2022
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Luis Rosado, 53, was convicted of first-degree murder for fatally stabbing his wife, Jillian Tatro, in North Adams.
Rosado was sentenced to life in prison without parole after the 2022 murder of Tatro, who was found with multiple stab wounds.
Tatro's son discovered her body after Rosado claimed she left with a friend; evidence linked Rosado to the crime.

title: State officials release 4 submissions to immigration agent misconduct portal launched in March
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: State officials released four reports from the ICE misconduct portal launched in March in response to a public records request.
The released reports include incidents at Logan Airport, Cohasset, Corporation Beach, and Needham High School involving alleged ICE misconduct.
Governor Healey's office did not disclose the total number of submissions received since the portal's launch.

title: Weighing pros, cons of Celtics trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Celtics are considering a trade for Giannis Antetokounmpo to address roster weaknesses identified by Brad Stevens.
A trade for Antetokounmpo might require parting with star Jaylen Brown, posing a significant decision for Celtics management.
Giannis Antetokounmpo's age and injury history are potential drawbacks for the Celtics, despite his elite playing ability.

title: Harvard College faculty to vote on limiting number of As for students
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Harvard faculty to vote on capping A grades at 20% plus four per class to address grade inflation.
The proposal emerged from a report acknowledging over 60% of grades awarded at Harvard are As.
Critics argue the cap fosters competition and raises ethical concerns about subjective grading.

title: Truck driver dies days after crash on North Attleborough highway
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Julio Flores from Pawtucket, Rhode Island, died nearly a week after a crash on I-95 South in North Attleborough.
The crash involved Flores' semi-truck and a Volkswagen Jetta which was found pinned against a guardrail.
Massachusetts State Police are investigating the incident; the status of the Jetta's four occupants remains undisclosed.

title: Sports Hub’s Mike Felger shares wild Red Sox trade rumor involving Mike Trout
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Mike Felger shared a rumor about the Red Sox's interest in trading for Angels' star Mike Trout.
Trout, an 11-time All-Star, is 34, with recent injury issues and a high salary of over $37 million annually.
Potential Red Sox trade package could involve Jarren Duran and Brayan Bello, both underperforming this season.

title: Romeo Doubs on learning Josh McDaniels’ offense: ‘I can already see how he thinks’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Romeo Doubs joins the Patriots and adjusts to learning Josh McDaniels' offensive system after leaving the Green Bay Packers.
Doubs praised Patriots quarterback Drake Maye for his energy and looks forward to developing chemistry with him.
Despite the Patriots' interest in AJ Brown, Doubs is open to various roles and acknowledges the team’s winning culture.

title: DA: Mass. man walked into police station, told officers he killed his father
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: David Boucher, 48, charged with first degree murder after confessing to killing his father in North Adams, Massachusetts.
The victim, identified as David A. Boucher, was found stabbed repeatedly in a home on Walnut St.
Boucher pleaded not guilty and will appear in court on June 12 for a pretrial hearing.

title: Worcester man, 62, killed by vehicle while crossing street, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: James McGuire, 62, was fatally struck by a vehicle on Sunday night near 137 Hope Ave in Worcester.
The incident occurred shortly after 9 p.m., and McGuire later died at a nearby hospital from his injuries.
Worcester police are investigating the crash; no driver details or potential charges have been released.
